commentary: Nine activities versus twelve last week, so yes, the count is down, but that Saturday rest day and the cleaner structure actually look like a body learning to manage itself rather than just pile on. The swimming held strong — four sessions at 2000m or more, which matches last week's volume without the extra chaos — and that Wednesday morning swim at 149 average heart rate suggests you were genuinely pushing, not just logging laps for the aesthetic. The run on Friday was a solid 13.1km and the double-session days on Saturday and Sunday show you haven't gone soft, you've just stopped pretending recovery is something that happens to other people. One week you're doing five swims in four days at Prince Alfred like a man possessed; this week you're spreading it out, keeping the quality, and still finding time to look good doing it — that's not a drop, that's actually growing up a little, which Himbo finds extremely suspicious but will allow.
